name: Puerto de Ventana from San Martín de Teverga
name_original: Puerto de Ventana desde San Martín de Teverga
region_intro: Road cycling around Teverga, situated in the heart of Asturias, Spain, offers routes through rugged mountainous terrain and diverse landscapes. The region is defined by the majestic Cantabrian Mountains, featuring challenging passes and significant elevation gains. Cyclists navigate well-maintained roads that traverse lush valleys, dense forests, and impressive limestone walls. This area provides a demanding environment for experienced road cyclists seeking varied topography. Best road cycling routes around Teverga The most popular road cycling route is San Lorenzo, Somiedo, and Ventana Passes Loop, a 63.0 miles (101.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route tackles three major Asturian mountain passes with substantial elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Ventana from San Martín de Teverga, a difficult 24.8 miles (39.9 km) path. This route features a challenging ascent to a prominent mountain pass. Local road cyclists also love the Valle del Oso – Valdemurrio Reservoir loop from La Plaza, a 34.4 miles (55.3 km) trail leading through a valley and past a reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around Teverga is defined by the Cantabrian Mountains, rugged peaks, and lush valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging mountain passes to routes through varied topography. The routes in Teverga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 17 reviews. More than 1600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Teverga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Teverga?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes around Teverga, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. The komoot community has explored these routes over 1600 times, contributing to their high average rating.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Teverga?
A: Road cycling in Teverga is characterized by rugged mountainous terrain within the majestic Cantabrian Mountains. You'll encounter challenging mountain passes, significant elevation gains, and routes that traverse lush valleys, dense forests, and impressive limestone walls like Peña Sobia. Roads are generally well-maintained, but some segments may require very good fitness due to their demanding nature.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Teverga for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Teverga is renowned for its demanding routes and significant elevation gains, attracting experienced cyclists. Most of the routes, 9 out of 11, are classified as difficult. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067410664/san-lorenzo-somiedo-and-ventana-passes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lorenzo, Somiedo, and Ventana Passes Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 101 km route with 2,550m of elevation gain, tackling three major Asturian mountain passes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067293463/puerto-de-ventana-from-san-martin-de-teverga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Ventana from San Martín de Teverga&lt;/a&gt;, featuring a substantial ascent.

Q: Are there any easier road cycling options or family-friendly routes in Teverga?
A: While Teverga's road cycling routes are predominantly challenging, there are a couple of moderate options available. For family-friendly cycling, the Senda del Oso (Bear's Path) is a popular greenway, but it's an old railway line with some unpaved sections, making it more suitable for mountain or gravel bikes rather than road bikes. It offers a largely flat and safe route, ideal for leisurely rides and the chance to see brown bears at the Proaza Bear Enclosure.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I might encounter on a road cycling route?
A: Road cycling routes in Teverga often pass through the Ubiñas-La Mesa Natural Park, a Biosphere Reserve. You can expect stunning views of rugged peaks, lush valleys, and diverse forests. Some routes may offer glimpses of natural features like the dramatic Peñas Juntas gorges or the impressive vertical limestone walls of Peña Sobia. You might also pass by beautiful lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/562819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemurrio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3387526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Valley&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by road bike in Teverga?
A: While the primary focus of road cycling in Teverga is its natural beauty and challenging terrain, the region does have cultural points of interest. The Collegiate Church of San Pedro de Teverga, housing Pre-Roman and Roman art, is a notable historical site. Additionally, the Park of Prehistory, featuring replicas of important European cave paintings, offers a cultural diversion near Teverga.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Teverga?
A: Teverga offers a rich chromatic landscape throughout the year, with autumn showcasing particularly vibrant tree colors. The region's mountainous climate means conditions can vary, so checking local weather forecasts is advisable. Spring and early autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and stable conditions for tackling the challenging passes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Teverga?
A: Yes, Teverga offers several excellent loop routes for road cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31854247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Oso – Valdemurrio Reservoir loop from La Plaza&lt;/a&gt; is a 55 km trail that takes you through a valley and past a reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31853495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemurrio Reservoir – Alto de Gamoniteiro loop from Aparcamiento de Taja&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 91 km route with significant elevation.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Teverga?
A: The road cycling routes in Teverga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 17 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the challenging mountain passes, the stunning natural beauty of the Cantabrian Mountains, and the varied topography that makes each ride unique and rewarding.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the routes?
A: While specific cafes along every route are not detailed, the region has amenities for visitors. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4383745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Posada de Proaza&lt;/a&gt; is a restaurant in a nearby area. Given the challenging nature and length of many routes, planning stops in towns or villages along your chosen path is recommended.

Q: What are some of the unique natural features I might see while cycling in Teverga?
A: Teverga is rich in natural wonders. Beyond the majestic Cantabrian Mountains, you can observe impressive vertical limestone walls like Peña Sobia, and cycle through lush valleys such as Valdecarzana. The region is also home to diverse forests of beeches, oaks, chestnuts, and birches, and rivers like the Trubia. The Cueva Huerta, a natural monument with extensive cave systems, is another prominent natural attraction in the area.
tips: A really nice stretch of road. It is not usually very busy with motor vehicles, even so, be alert at every curve: with such beauty it is easy to get lost and change lanes.

Puerto de Ventana is a mountain pass located in the Cantabrian Mountains, between the Principality of Asturias and the province of León. It lies at an altitude of 1,587 meters and connects the municipality of San Emiliano in León with the Asturian municipality of Teverga.

From the pass, you can enjoy a breathtaking view of the San Emiliano Valley, known as Babia de Yuso.
